creative_intent.purpose: Let judges audit their own game evidence and receive an inspectable boundary-aware receipt.
creative_intent.audience: Developers and hackathon judges evaluating AI-built games.
creative_intent.voice_or_art_direction: Forensic editorial interface; evidence ledger rather than generic analytics dashboard.
directions_explored: judge-supplied live audit plus CI gate

human_accountability.compatibility_note: Legacy field name; responsible_editor is the authorized agent final validator unless an exact external requirement requires a human actor.
human_accountability.responsible_editor: Codex main conductor and final validator
human_accountability.external_human_action_basis: No real-person, regulated, or high-impact claim; contest rules require truthful Codex provenance rather than human-only approval.
material_edits_or_acceptance_rationale: Added credential-free 64 KiB JSON audit with no submission history.
Added copy/download receipt handoff and visible audit trail.
Added dependency-free GitHub Action sharing the same audit engine.

disclosure_assessment.actor_role: ordinary creation-tool user and hackathon entrant
contract_or_client_requirements: Demo video must explain Codex and GPT-5.6 use.
Submission must provide the primary Codex Session ID.
disclosure_assessment.material_deception_risk: Low; the repository and submission explicitly state Codex and GPT-5.6 provenance and preserve pre-existing evidence boundaries.
disclosure_assessment.decision_rationale: Build Week requires explicit Codex/GPT-5.6 collaboration evidence; README and submission materials carry that disclosure. No additional generic AI label is required inside the product UI.
disclosure_assessment.public_label_or_control_applied: Codex/GPT-5.6 provenance in README, demo narration, and Devpost submission fields.
disclosure_assessment.machine_readable_provenance: Git history, primary Codex Session ID, and internal release record.

remaining_defects: Independent Claude re-review pending after weekly limit resets at 20:00 KST.
